message:
⚡ Progressive stack discovery: split bulk deployment query into per-namespace batches (#673)

The bulk `kubectl get deployments -A -o json` returned 4+ MB on clusters with
many namespaces, causing WebSocket timeouts/failures. The stack selector was
stuck at 13 stacks while the Stack Monitor (which uses a different hook) showed
86 components.

Two-phase progressive discovery:
- Phase 1 (fast): labeled pods + InferencePools + shared resources → immediate
  UI update with pod-based stacks
- Phase 2 (progressive): filter namespaces by isLlmdNamespace(), query
  deployments per-namespace in batches of 3, add new stacks to UI as each
  batch completes

Combined with the existing localStorage cache, users see cached stacks
instantly, Phase 1 refreshes them, and Phase 2 progressively fills in
deployment-only namespaces. Each per-namespace query returns ~5-50 KB
instead of 4+ MB.

Also extracted helper functions (buildComponentsFromDeployments, sortStacks,
detectAutoscaler, buildInfraComponents) to reduce duplication and moved
HPAResource/WVAResource/VPAResource interfaces to module level.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
